root_text.an4.96:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo attahitāya paṭipanno hoti no parahitāya?
root_text.an4.96:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo attanā rāgavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, no paraṁ rāgavinayāya samādapeti;
root_text.an4.96:2.3: attanā dosavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, no paraṁ dosavinayāya samādapeti;
root_text.an4.96:2.4: attanā mohavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, no paraṁ mohavinayāya samādapeti.
root_text.an4.96:2.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāya.
root_text.an4.96:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo parahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no attahitāya?
root_text.an4.96:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo attanā na rāgavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, paraṁ rāgavinayāya samādapeti;
root_text.an4.96:3.3: attanā na dosavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, paraṁ dosavinayāya samādapeti;
root_text.an4.96:3.4: attanā na mohavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, paraṁ mohavinayāya samādapeti.
root_text.an4.96:3.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo parahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no attahitāya.
root_text.an4.96:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo nevattahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāya?
root_text.an4.96:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo attanā na rāgavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, no paraṁ rāgavinayāya samādapeti;
root_text.an4.96:4.3: attanā na dosavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, no paraṁ dosavinayāya samādapeti;
root_text.an4.96:4.4: attanā na mohavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, no paraṁ mohavinayāya samādapeti.
root_text.an4.96:4.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo nevattahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāya.
root_text.an4.96:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo attahitāya ceva paṭipanno hoti parahitāya ca?
root_text.an4.96:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo attanā ca rāgavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, parañca rāgavinayāya samādapeti;
root_text.an4.96:5.3: attanā ca dosavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, parañca dosavinayāya samādapeti;
root_text.an4.96:5.4: attanā ca mohavinayāya paṭipanno hoti, parañca mohavinayāya samādapeti.
root_text.an4.96:5.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo attahitāya ceva paṭipanno hoti parahitāya ca.
root_text.an4.96:5.6: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.96:1.1: “Mendicants, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.96:1.3: One who practices to benefit themselves, but not others;
translation_text.an4.96:1.4: one who practices to benefit others, but not themselves;
translation_text.an4.96:1.5: one who practices to benefit neither themselves nor others; and
translation_text.an4.96:1.6: one who practices to benefit both themselves and others.
translation_text.an4.96:2.1: And how does an individual practice to benefit themselves, but not others?
translation_text.an4.96:2.2: It’s when an individual practices to remove their own greed, hate, and delusion, but doesn’t encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an4.96:2.5: That’s how an individual practices to benefit themselves, but not others.
translation_text.an4.96:3.1: And how does an individual practice to benefit others, but not themselves?
translation_text.an4.96:3.2: It’s when an individual doesn’t practice to remove their own greed, hate, and delusion, but encourages others to remove theirs.
translation_text.an4.96:3.5: That’s how an individual practices to benefit others, but not themselves.
translation_text.an4.96:4.1: And how does an individual practice to benefit neither themselves nor others?
translation_text.an4.96:4.2: It’s when an individual doesn’t practice to remove their own greed, hate, and delusion, nor do they encourage others to remove theirs.
translation_text.an4.96:4.5: That’s how an individual practices to benefit neither themselves nor others.
translation_text.an4.96:5.1: And how does an individual practice to benefit both themselves and others?
translation_text.an4.96:5.2: It’s when an individual practices to remove their own greed, hate, and delusion, and encourages others to remove theirs.
translation_text.an4.96:5.5: That’s how an individual practices to benefit both themselves and others.
translation_text.an4.96:5.6: These are the four individuals found in the world.”
